frameworks.name: recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: cortical circuit model coupling categorical choice and continuous estimate

frameworks.name: confirmation bias
frameworks.one_line: post-decision bias whose neural origin the model targets

core_question: What neural-circuit mechanisms give rise to post-decision biases in perception, and are categorical choices and continuous estimates computed in a common cortical circuit?
key_assumptions: post-decision biases reflect circuit-level dynamics, not just readout
categorical choice and continuous estimate may share a single circuit
manipulations: network architecture supporting both categorical and continuous outputs
key_findings: categorical choice induces a choice-dependent confirmation bias in the continuous estimate
categorical choice produces an overall decrease in sensitivity to subsequent sensory evidence
interpretations: a single neural network model can account for both post-decision biases simultaneously
